content:
Hello! I'm currently working on the French translation and I've come across one little problem because French has two “you.” I know it's an issue with other languages too, I've seen questions about it, but I couldn't find anything (sorry if it exists and I missed it <:aurita_beg2:1407642015428116541>) about how the devs would like to address the player

Just in case, here's how our “you” work:

1) **Vous:** Most often used to show respect to the other person but can also be used to create some distance, mark your boundaries (with a stranger or even someone you dislike)

2) **Tu:** Most often used with friends/family but can also be used with someone you often see (like your colleagues). In rarer cases, it can be used to show disrespect to someone you’re expected to use “vous” with (like your superior). It's also very common for people to ask “can I use *tu* to talk to you?” even if you aren't very close to them, just because “tu” is warmer, friendlier

Up until this point, I was translating “you” in the UI as “tu” because for me Mori's staff is indeed warm and friendly (we also don't get letters like Light does in other games) and it felt right to me as a player. But I realized that I should ask what is the devs preference in this case <:karen_nervous:1408324291237511259> 

So, should I use the “formal you” (vous) or the “informal you” (tu) to address the player? <:murphy_cake:1408324295180030002>
thread_title: How to address the player (formal vs informal)

content:
Well I think this is the local language matters where you have the final decision.
For me, Vietnamese also have a lot of way to address people. But we ended up with “bạn”, which means “you” (yeah of course), but just because Vietnamese address people formally with respect like that a lot (from a long time ago), we feel natural using it for UI (in Genshin impact as well).
thread_title: How to address the player (formal vs informal)

content: So yeah its boiled down to your culture.
thread_title: How to address the player (formal vs informal)

content: And stick to the question, my language use the formal you.
thread_title: How to address the player (formal vs informal)

content: (Refer to other games might be a good idea.)
thread_title: How to address the player (formal vs informal)

content:
I feel like French is more complicated because the use of “you” is, to be honest, more based on “vibes” than anything (yes, it's as annoying as it sounds). Like your boss may say to you to use the “informal you” to address them but then you'll meet someone in an informal setting (like a friend who introduced them to you) who's very polite (or very cold) and will use the “formal you” to address you, things like that 
French translations for games are a little like that too. There's a general rule where big studios usually use the “formal you” for the player and small studios/indies may use the “informal you” but it's not set in stone either. So I think it's more about the image BIAV wants to give to the French players; more polite but maybe a little cold or more familiar but warmer/friendlier <:murphy_cake:1408324295180030002>
thread_title: How to address the player (formal vs informal)
